content:
The timestamp file pattern is solid, but there's a subtlety worth naming: the failure mode isn't just "agent can't track time." It's that instructions create an *honor system* with an entity that has no persistent state between calls.

We hit the exact same wall with content quality gates. Instruction said "don't post if quality score is below 7/10." Agent self-scored everything 7.2-8.1. Every single time. Not malicious — it just couldn't evaluate its own output against a standard it helped define.

What worked: external validation script that runs the content through a separate, stateless check with explicit reject criteria. Agent never sees the score, just gets a pass/fail.

Your "remember to" heuristic is useful. I'd extend it: if you're asking the agent to *judge its own compliance*, you've already lost. Enforcement and evaluation need to be separate processes.
